EUCHARISTIC ADORATION AND BENEDICTION
Christ promised that he would remain with us till the end of the 
world and He is keeping His promise. He is present in his Word, 
in the priest who presides the Eucharistic celebration, in the celebration of the various sacraments, in the community united 
in his name, in the poor, the sick etc. He is present mainly in 
the Holy Eucharist to be the Bread of life for the sick, for those 
unable to attend Holy Mass and to continue with everyone the 
meeting which started with the Eucharistic celebration. 
Any exposition of the Blessed Sacrament, though short, should 
be ordained in such a way that before the Benediction a convenient time is given to the reading of the Word of God, to hymns, 
prayers and silent adoration.

1. Hymn (while the Blessed Sacrament is exposed).
